author: tuyen4818
content:
> The chain split at slot 22688 because Gloas / ePBS envelope propagation broke — the new layer where the beacon block and its e
in lodestar forkchoice debug (both lodestar_nethermind_2 and lodestar_ethrex_1), finalized checkpoint happen later at epoch 710 which is slot 22720
so 22688 is not the chain split, it was pruned in our forkchoice data because it's pre-finalized

conclusion

22800
├─ FULL  -> 22801 (majority)
└─ EMPTY -> 22820 (prysm-nethermind-1)

so somehow `prysm-nethermind-1` reorged 12 blocks and decided to build on 22800 EMPTY, instead of FULL like the majority

author: tuyen4818
content:
found an issue with `lodestar-ethrex-2`, it produced orphaned blocks
- slot `22903 FULL` is head
- `shouldBuildOnFull()` returned false due to ptc votes, so node produced on `22903 EMPTY`
- the produced block does not become head because `22903 FULL` already won and keeps being the head there
- so it was orphaned, happened both with 22929 and 22930

I think that's a design gap of `should_build_on_full()` when it conflicts to `get_head()`, would be great if someone can confirm https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/issues/5307
not sure if it's the same issue to `prysm-nethermind-1` above

author: barnabasbusa
content: do people think we can try to recover this bad chain?

author: tuyen4818
content:
the main thing of a devnet is to find client bugs, or spec bugs, or client interop bugs
I feel we have enough data to investigate, looks like we already have a spec issue here (if not, it's a lodestar issue that we need to fix first before retry)
need to take some time for people to investigate first before decide if we should recover the chain or go with a new one (with new spec)

author: potuz
content: I'm analyzing logs Prysm indeed never gets the payload for 22800, also some weird stuff with timings may be affecting forkchoice, we are getting the PTC attestations **before** the beacon block (so therefore before the payload) right at the beginning of the slot, we do not queue these ones when we don't have a block, so this may be leading to a lot of reorgs if people are reorging blocks based on PTC (Prysm does not do this yet, I am waiting for stable images from clients to test this)

author: potuz
content: So Lodestar for sure is sending PTC attestations very early, if every client does this then it may happen that PTC ends up being voting for empty when they intended full

author: potuz
content: I meant the actual blocks, I want to see the attestations, but I think Prysm node still has them so I can check myself here

author: potuz
content: but yes, the problem is that Prysm can't even sync 22801 because it does not receive the parent payload

author: potuz
content: Prysm is completely isolated at the time and doesn't get anything from there on cause it can't get the payload

author: potuz
content: it Only gets data when it falls back to init sync, we may have a P2P issue <@363800010518822915> or probably the peering in the devnet is still fucked up. But from the point of view of this node, it's behavior is correct

content: <@575994543527297055> can you check if lodestar-nethermind-1 was active at the moment? this was the only active peer that Prysm had and it returns an empty stream when we request the payload by root
